tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zdecyduj, ktore zdania (1.1-1.8) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information).
tasks.text:
POLAR BEARS

Polar bears are the largest land carnivores on Earth. Adult males can weigh between 400 and 700 kilograms and stand up to 3 metres tall when on their hind legs. Despite their enormous size, polar bears are surprisingly good swimmers and have been spotted swimming in open water more than 100 kilometres from the nearest land.

These impressive animals live in the Arctic region, mainly in Canada, Russia, Norway, Greenland, and the United States (Alaska). They spend much of their time on sea ice, which they use as a platform for hunting seals — their primary food source. Polar bears have an extraordinary sense of smell and can detect a seal on the ice from nearly one kilometre away. They often wait patiently beside holes in the ice for hours, ready to catch a seal when it comes up to breathe.

Although polar bears appear white, their fur is actually transparent and hollow. Each hair is a clear tube that reflects light, making the bear look white and helping it blend in with the snow and ice. Underneath their fur, polar bears have black skin, which helps them absorb heat from the sun. They also have a thick layer of fat, up to 11 centimetres thick, that keeps them warm in temperatures that can drop below minus 40 degrees Celsius.

Female polar bears usually give birth to twins in snow dens during the winter months. The newborn cubs are tiny — they weigh only about 600 grams, which is roughly the weight of a loaf of bread. The mother stays in the den with her cubs for several months, living off her stored body fat while nursing them.

Polar bears are classified as a vulnerable species. The biggest threat to their survival is climate change, which is causing Arctic sea ice to melt earlier in the spring and form later in the autumn. This gives polar bears less time to hunt on the ice, and many bears are losing weight and struggling to feed their young. Scientists estimate that there are currently between 22,000 and 31,000 polar bears left in the wild.

items.statement: Polar bears are the biggest meat-eating animals that live on land.
Polar bears can only be found in Canada and Russia.
Polar bears mainly hunt fish in the Arctic Ocean.
The fur of a polar bear is actually colourless rather than white.
Polar bears have white skin under their fur.
A newborn polar bear cub weighs approximately 600 grams.
Male polar bears help to look after the cubs in the den.
Polar bears have been successfully kept in zoos around the world for over a century.
